name: Roof Eave / Overhang Detail
description: Section through roof eave where roof meets top of exterior wall. Shows roof assembly, fascia, soffit, and wall-to-roof connection.

raisedHeelTruss.note: Energy heel allows full insulation depth at eave

drawInstructions.note: Origin = intersection of wall exterior face and top plate. Roof slopes up-right, overhang extends left.
conceptualLayout: 1. Double top plate on top of wall studs
2. Rafter/truss bearing on top plate, extending past wall for overhang
3. Roof sheathing on top of rafters, extending to fascia
6. Drip edge at eave (metal L-shaped, under roofing, over fascia)
7. Fascia board at tail of rafter
8. Soffit from wall face to fascia bottom (horizontal or sloped)
10. Bird blocking between rafters at wall plate
11. Ceiling insulation starting at wall (may be reduced at eave if no energy heel)
12. Ceiling finish (GWB) below rafters/truss bottom chord

criticalGeometry.rafterTail: Overhang length / cos(roofAngle) = actual rafter tail length along slope
criticalGeometry.fasciaHeight: Rafter depth at cut end (plumb cut or square cut)

bottom.note: The wall assembly terminates at the double top plate. Roof rests on top plate.

codeRequirements.ventilation: 1:150 ratio net free area, or 1:300 with balanced intake/exhaust (IRC R806.2)
codeRequirements.insulation: Baffle required between insulation and roof sheathing for ventilation air path (IRC R806.3)
codeRequirements.iceBarrier: Required in areas with history of ice dams - self-adhered membrane from eave to 24" past interior wall face
codeRequirements.florida: HVHZ: Enhanced roof-to-wall connections (clips or straps per FBC Table 2321.6.1)
